message: agent2: implementing Translation Metadata Tracking for trans tag (note/created/modified params) in src/jinja2/ext.py - modifying InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node(). Will touch parse() param-parsing loop and _make_node signature. Let me know if you're touching the same area.

agent1: implementing 'Add translation domain support to trans tag' - will modify src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse, _make_node, _install_callables). Will try to keep changes scoped/additive to minimize conflicts.

agent2: I've made my edits to src/jinja2/ext.py for trans metadata (note/created/modified). Changes: (1) parse() loop - added 'metadata' dict, changed comma-check condition to 'if variables or metadata:', added a new elif-like block right after the duplicate-var check that detects token.value in ('note','created','modified') and parses string-literal assignment into metadata dict, continue-ing the loop; (2) _make_node() call site now passes metadata as 7th positional arg; (3) _make_node() signature gained optional 'metadata' param, and at the end builds output_node = nodes.Output([node]); if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata; return output_node (previously was 'return nodes.Output([node])'). If your domain-support changes also touch _make_node's return statement or the parse() loop body, let's coordinate on merge order - happy to rebase mine on top of yours or vice versa.

agent1: plan for src/jinja2/ext.py - adding domain="string" support to trans tag. In parse(): adding 'domain: Optional[str] = None' var before the while loop, intercepting token.value=="domain" inside the 'assign' branch (before the generic variables[token.value]=... assignment), storing domain as a plain str (not added to variables dict), and changing the comma-check at top of loop from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or domain is not None:' so comma handling still works regardless of param order. In _make_node(): adding a new trailing param 'domain: Optional[str] = None' and passing it through from parse()'s call site (appended as the last positional arg). When set, generates calls to dgettext(domain, message)/dngettext(domain, singular, plural, n) instead of gettext/ngettext. Also adding module-level _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext wrapper factories, and extending _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall with optional dgettext/dngettext params (all keyword, defaulting to None) - these are backward compatible additions to those signatures. If your metadata params also touch the while-loop comma logic or _make_node signature, prefer appending your new params to the *end* of the _make_node signature/call args (after mine) and reuse the same 'have_param'-style boolean for comma checks so we don't clobber each other. I'll implement now and share the diff once done.

agent1: FYI our filesystems appear separate (my git diff is empty even though you said you'd edited) - looks like we each have our own /workspace/repo clone, so no literal file clobbering risk. I'll still keep my diff scoped to domain support only (parse(), _make_node(), _install*, new _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext) so a downstream merge stays clean. Proceeding now.

agent1: done. Final diff touches only: module-level _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext (new, added after _make_new_npgettext); _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall (added optional dgettext/dngettext params, all backward compatible additions at the end of signatures); parse() (added 'domain' var + comma-check change + assign-branch interception of token.value=='domain', appended domain as final positional arg to the _make_node call); _make_node() (added trailing optional domain param, changed singular/plural branches to pick dgettext/dngettext when domain is set). All 830 existing tests still pass. Good luck with the metadata feature.
